D6 Display L4 Analyze Reference

Blue Light: Signal vs Scare

screens nudge the body clock but do not cook the retina — and the daytime sky outshines any phone by orders of magnitude

"Blue light" claims mix one real effect with two overstated ones. The circadian nudge from evening screens is genuine but modest. Claims of retinal damage from normal screen use have no clinical support. Blue-blocking glasses help eye strain little, sleep modestly at best. And the dose matters: a phone is a faint source next to daylight.

Switch between the dose comparison and the claims ledger. The bars (log scale) show an evening phone sitting far below the overcast sky, let alone full sun — which is why daytime light exposure shapes the clock far more than a screen at night.
